findings.title: S3 account-level Block Public Access ignores public ACLs and restricts public buckets
findings.description: **Amazon S3** account-level **Block Public Access** is assessed for `ignore_public_acls` and `restrict_public_buckets` to confirm centralized blocking of ACL-based public access and limiting buckets with public policies to in-account principals.
findings.recommendation: Turn on account-level **Block Public Access** (prefer enabling all four: `block_public_acls`, `ignore_public_acls`, `block_public_policy`, `restrict_public_buckets`) to enforce least privilege. For legitimate access, use private buckets with **CloudFront**, VPC endpoints, or presigned URLs. Regularly review policies with IAM Access Analyzer.

findings.title: S3 bucket has bucket ACLs disabled
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **Object Ownership** set to `BucketOwnerEnforced`, which disables bucket and object ACLs. Buckets using any other ownership setting indicate that ACLs remain enabled.
findings.recommendation: Disable ACLs by setting **Object Ownership** to `BucketOwnerEnforced` and manage access with **IAM** and **bucket policies** under **least privilege**. Centralize authorization, review policies regularly, and use organizational guardrails to prevent re-enabling ACLs. *Migrate ACL-based grants into policies before the change.*

findings.recommendation: Disable ACLs by setting **Object Ownership** to `BucketOwnerEnforced` and manage access with **IAM** and **bucket policies** under **least privilege**. Centralize authorization, review policies regularly, and use organizational guardrails to prevent re-enabling ACLs. *Migrate ACL-based grants into policies before the change.*

findings.title: S3 bucket policy does not allow cross-account access
findings.description:
**Amazon S3 bucket policies** are analyzed for statements that grant **cross-account access**.

Any policy that names principals outside the owning account (other account IDs or `Principal: "*"`) is treated as cross-account; absence of a policy implies no cross-account grants.
findings.recommendation:
Enforce **least privilege**: limit bucket policy `Principal` to your account or approved org IDs with fixed values; avoid wildcards.

Use **role-based cross-account access** with scoped permissions when needed. Add **defense-in-depth** conditions (private networks, TLS), and periodically review for unintended external access.

findings.title: S3 bucket has cross-region replication configured to a bucket in a different region
findings.description:
**Amazon S3 buckets** use **cross-Region replication** with `versioning` and an enabled rule that targets a destination bucket in a different AWS Region.

Buckets with same-Region targets, missing destinations, or disabled `versioning` don't meet this replication posture.
findings.recommendation:
Enable **CRR** to a different Region with `versioning` and least-privilege roles.

- Replicate needed prefixes and metadata
- Consider `S3 Replication Time Control` for tighter RPO
- Protect deletes via `delete marker` strategy and Object Lock
- Monitor replication metrics and test DR regularly

Align with **defense in depth** and availability by design.

findings.title: S3 bucket has default server-side encryption (SSE) enabled
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** have a default **server-side encryption** setting that automatically encrypts new objects using `SSE-S3` or `SSE-KMS`. This evaluates whether a bucket has a default encryption configuration defined.
findings.recommendation: Enable default encryption on all buckets, preferring `SSE-KMS` for sensitive data to retain key control and auditing. Enforce encryption with restrictive bucket policies, apply **least privilege** to KMS keys with rotation, and re-encrypt existing objects. Use **defense in depth** monitoring to detect drift and noncompliant uploads.

findings.title: S3 bucket has event notifications enabled
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** define a **notification configuration** that publishes bucket events (for example `s3:ObjectCreated:*`, `s3:ObjectRemoved:*`) to a destination. The evaluation identifies buckets that lack any notification setup.
findings.recommendation:
Enable **S3 event notifications** for relevant events (e.g., `s3:ObjectCreated:*`, `s3:ObjectRemoved:*`) and route to controlled destinations (SNS, SQS, Lambda, EventBridge).

Use prefix/suffix filters, avoid recursive triggers, and enforce **least privilege** on targets. Pair with object-level logging for **defense in depth**.

findings.title: S3 bucket has server-side encryption with AWS KMS
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** use server-side encryption with **AWS KMS** keys, including dual-layer `aws:kms:dsse`. The evaluation identifies buckets whose default encryption is `aws:kms` or `aws:kms:dsse` rather than SSE-S3.
findings.recommendation: Enable default **SSE-KMS** (or **DSSE-KMS** for highly sensitive data). Use a customer-managed key, enforce **least privilege** and separation of duties for key usage, and require KMS encryption via bucket policy (specify `aws:kms` and a designated key). Monitor key activity in **CloudTrail** and consider **S3 Bucket Keys** to control cost.

findings.title: S3 bucket has Block Public Access with IgnorePublicAcls and RestrictPublicBuckets enabled at bucket or account level
findings.description:
**Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **Block Public Access** settings, ensuring `ignore_public_acls` and `restrict_public_buckets` are enabled at the bucket or account scope.

*Account-wide protections, when present, are treated as effective for the bucket.*
findings.recommendation: Enable **Block Public Access** at account and bucket levels with `block_public_acls`, `ignore_public_acls`, `block_public_policy`, and `restrict_public_buckets` set to `true`. Apply **least privilege** and **defense in depth**. *If public access is required*, narrowly scope policies to fixed principals and conditions.

findings.title: S3 bucket has a lifecycle configuration enabled
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** use **Lifecycle configurations** with at least one rule `Status: Enabled` to automate object `Transitions` and `Expiration` based on age, prefix, or tags
findings.recommendation: Define **Lifecycle policies** by data classification: set `Expiration` to enforce retention, use `Transitions` to lower-cost classes, and enable `AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload`. For critical logs, keep versioning and, *if required*, **Object Lock**. Limit who can change lifecycle using least privilege and separation of duties.

findings.title: S3 bucket has MFA Delete enabled
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are assessed for **MFA Delete** status. MFA Delete requires a second factor to permanently delete object versions or change `Versioning` configuration. The finding highlights buckets where this protection is not enabled.
findings.recommendation: Enable **MFA Delete** on sensitive, versioned buckets so permanent deletions and `Versioning` changes require a second factor. Apply **least privilege** to restrict version purge actions, enforce **change control**, and combine with **Object Lock** or immutable backups for defense in depth.

findings.title: S3 bucket has Object Lock enabled
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** have **Object Lock** enabled at the bucket level, applying WORM controls to object versions
findings.recommendation:
Enable **Object Lock** for critical data and set appropriate default retention in `GOVERNANCE` or `COMPLIANCE` mode.

Apply **immutability** and **least privilege** by restricting permissions that bypass retention, and use legal holds when you need indefinite protection for investigations or regulatory requirements.

findings.title: S3 bucket has object versioning enabled
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **object versioning** being `Enabled`, which maintains multiple versions of the same object key for historical state retention
findings.recommendation:
Enable **S3 versioning** for buckets holding important or shared data.
- Enforce **least privilege** to limit delete/overwrite
- Use **Object Lock** and/or **MFA Delete** for stronger protection
- Apply **lifecycle rules** to manage noncurrent versions and costs
- Layer with backups/replication for **defense in depth**

findings.title: S3 bucket policy does not allow public write access
findings.description: **Amazon S3 bucket policies** are evaluated for **public write permissions** (e.g., `s3:PutObject`, `s3:Delete*`, or `s3:*`). Account or bucket **Public Access Block** that restricts public buckets is considered when determining exposure.
findings.recommendation: Restrict writes to trusted principals using **least privilege**; avoid `Principal: "*"`. Enable **Public Access Block** at account and bucket levels for defense in depth. Prefer IAM roles over broad bucket policies, require private access paths, and enable versioning to recover from unwanted changes.

findings.title: S3 bucket is not publicly accessible to Everyone or Authenticated Users
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **public access** via ACLs and bucket policies. The check identifies account or bucket `PublicAccessBlock` protections (`IgnorePublicAcls`, `RestrictPublicBuckets`) and flags buckets granting group access to `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers`, or with a public bucket policy.
findings.recommendation: Enforce defense in depth: enable **S3 Block Public Access** at org/account and bucket levels (`BlockPublicAcls`, `IgnorePublicAcls`, `BlockPublicPolicy`, `RestrictPublicBuckets`). Apply **least privilege** with explicit principals; avoid ACLs via Object Ownership. Use private access patterns (e.g., CloudFront OAC or presigned URLs) and monitor with analyzers.

findings.title: S3 bucket is not publicly listable by Everyone or any authenticated AWS user
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **public listing via ACLs**. Grants of `READ`, `READ_ACP`, or `FULL_CONTROL` to the `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers` groups are identified. Effective **Block Public Access** at account or bucket level (notably `IgnorePublicAcls` and `RestrictPublicBuckets`) is considered in the evaluation.
findings.recommendation:
Enable account-level **S3 Block Public Access** (`BlockPublicAcls`, `IgnorePublicAcls`, `BlockPublicPolicy`, `RestrictPublicBuckets`).
- Remove ACL grants to `AllUsers`/`AuthenticatedUsers`; apply **least privilege** with IAM/bucket policies.
- Favor private patterns (VPC endpoints, CloudFront OAC, presigned URLs) and disable ACLs via Object Ownership.

findings.title: S3 bucket ACL does not grant write access to Everyone or any AWS customer
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are assessed for ACL grants that allow **public write** access to `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers` via `WRITE`, `WRITE_ACP`, or `FULL_CONTROL`. Effective **Block Public Access** at account or bucket level (`ignore_public_acls`, `restrict_public_buckets`) is considered.
findings.recommendation: Apply **least privilege** to S3 writes. Enable account-level **Block Public Access** and use **Object Ownership** to disable ACLs. Grant write only to fixed principals via bucket policies with tight conditions (e.g., org IDs, VPC endpoints). Add **versioning** and monitoring for defense-in-depth.

findings.title: S3 bucket policy denies requests over insecure transport
findings.description:
**Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for a bucket policy that enforces **secure transport** by denying requests when `aws:SecureTransport` is `false`.

Buckets without this explicit denial, or without a policy, are treated as allowing access over insecure transport.
findings.recommendation:
Enforce **HTTPS-only** access with a bucket policy that denies requests when `aws:SecureTransport=false`.

Prefer **private access** (VPC endpoints or CloudFront with TLS), avoid S3 website endpoints, apply **least privilege**, use short-lived HTTPS **pre-signed URLs**, and monitor logs for insecure access attempts.

findings.title: S3 bucket is not a known shadow resource owned by another account
findings.description:
**Amazon S3 buckets** using **predictable service naming** (e.g., `aws-glue-assets-<account>-<region>`, `sagemaker-<region>-<account>`) are identified and their **ownership** checked.

Buckets tied to your account that match these patterns but are owned by another account-across regions-are surfaced as shadow-resource candidates.
findings.recommendation:
Apply **defense in depth**:
- **Preprovision and own** required service buckets in all current and planned regions
- Enforce **least privilege** so services write only to approved bucket names/ARNs
- Use **non-guessable names** where you control naming
- Monitor for look-alike buckets and separate duties for bucket creation vs. use

Enable **S3 event notifications** for relevant events (e.g., `s3:ObjectCreated:*`, `s3:ObjectRemoved:*`) and route to controlled destinations (SNS, SQS, Lambda, EventBridge).

Use prefix/suffix filters, avoid recursive triggers, and enforce **least privilege** on targets. Pair with object-level logging for **defense in depth**.

findings.title: S3 bucket has server-side encryption with AWS KMS
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** use server-side encryption with **AWS KMS** keys, including dual-layer `aws:kms:dsse`. The evaluation identifies buckets whose default encryption is `aws:kms` or `aws:kms:dsse` rather than SSE-S3.
findings.recommendation: Enable default **SSE-KMS** (or **DSSE-KMS** for highly sensitive data). Use a customer-managed key, enforce **least privilege** and separation of duties for key usage, and require KMS encryption via bucket policy (specify `aws:kms` and a designated key). Monitor key activity in **CloudTrail** and consider **S3 Bucket Keys** to control cost.

findings.recommendation: Enable **Block Public Access** at account and bucket levels with `block_public_acls`, `ignore_public_acls`, `block_public_policy`, and `restrict_public_buckets` set to `true`. findings.title: S3 bucket policy does not allow public write access
findings.description: **Amazon S3 bucket policies** are evaluated for **public write permissions** (e.g., `s3:PutObject`, `s3:Delete*`, or `s3:*`). Account or bucket **Public Access Block** that restricts public buckets is considered when determining exposure.
findings.recommendation: Restrict writes to trusted principals using **least privilege**; avoid `Principal: "*"`. Enable **Public Access Block** at account and bucket levels for defense in depth. Prefer IAM roles over broad bucket policies, require private access paths, and enable versioning to recover from unwanted changes.

findings.title: S3 bucket is not publicly accessible to Everyone or Authenticated Users
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **public access** via ACLs and bucket policies. The check identifies account or bucket `PublicAccessBlock` protections (`IgnorePublicAcls`, `RestrictPublicBuckets`) and flags buckets granting group access to `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers`, or with a public bucket policy.
findings.recommendation: Enforce defense in depth: enable **S3 Block Public Access** at org/account and bucket levels (`BlockPublicAcls`, `IgnorePublicAcls`, `BlockPublicPolicy`, `RestrictPublicBuckets`). Apply **least privilege** with explicit principals; avoid ACLs via Object Ownership. Use private access patterns (e.g., CloudFront OAC or presigned URLs) and monitor with analyzers.

findings.title: S3 bucket is not publicly listable by Everyone or any authenticated AWS user
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **public listing via ACLs**. Grants of `READ`, `READ_ACP`, or `FULL_CONTROL` to the `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers` groups are identified. Effective **Block Public Access** at account or bucket level (notably `IgnorePublicAcls` and `RestrictPublicBuckets`) is considered in the evaluation.
findings.recommendation:
Enable account-level **S3 Block Public Access** (`BlockPublicAcls`, `IgnorePublicAcls`, `BlockPublicPolicy`, `RestrictPublicBuckets`).
- Remove ACL grants to `AllUsers`/`AuthenticatedUsers`; apply **least privilege** with IAM/bucket policies.
- Favor private patterns (VPC endpoints, CloudFront OAC, presigned URLs) and disable ACLs via Object Ownership.

findings.title: S3 bucket ACL does not grant write access to Everyone or any AWS customer
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are assessed for ACL grants that allow **public write** access to `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers` via `WRITE`, `WRITE_ACP`, or `FULL_CONTROL`. Effective **Block Public Access** at account or bucket level (`ignore_public_acls`, `restrict_public_buckets`) is considered.
findings.recommendation: Apply **least privilege** to S3 writes. Enable account-level **Block Public Access** and use **Object Ownership** to disable ACLs. Grant write only to fixed principals via bucket policies with tight conditions (e.g., org IDs, VPC endpoints). Add **versioning** and monitoring for defense-in-depth.

findings.title: S3 bucket policy denies requests over insecure transport
findings.description:
**Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for a bucket policy that enforces **secure transport** by denying requests when `aws:SecureTransport` is `false`.

Buckets without this explicit denial, or without a policy, are treated as allowing access over insecure transport.
findings.recommendation:
Enforce **HTTPS-only** access with a bucket policy that denies requests when `aws:SecureTransport=false`.

Prefer **private access** (VPC endpoints or CloudFront with TLS), avoid S3 website endpoints, apply **least privilege**, use short-lived HTTPS **pre-signed URLs**, and monitor logs for insecure access attempts.

findings.title: S3 bucket is not a known shadow resource owned by another account
findings.description:
**Amazon S3 buckets** using **predictable service naming** (e.g., `aws-glue-assets-<account>-<region>`, `sagemaker-<region>-<account>`) are identified and their **ownership** checked.

Buckets tied to your account that match these patterns but are owned by another account-across regions-are surfaced as shadow-resource candidates.
findings.recommendation:
Apply **defense in depth**:
- **Preprovision and own** required service buckets in all current and planned regions
- Enforce **least privilege** so services write only to approved bucket names/ARNs
- Use **non-guessable names** where you control naming
- Monitor for look-alike buckets and separate duties for bucket creation vs. use

findings.title: S3 bucket policy does not allow cross-account access
findings.description:
**Amazon S3 bucket policies** are analyzed for statements that grant **cross-account access**.

Any policy that names principals outside the owning account (other account IDs or `Principal: "*"`) is treated as cross-account; absence of a policy implies no cross-account grants.
findings.recommendation:
Enforce **least privilege**: limit bucket policy `Principal` to your account or approved org IDs with fixed values; avoid wildcards.

Use **role-based cross-account access** with scoped permissions when needed. Add **defense-in-depth** conditions (private networks, TLS), and periodically review for unintended external access.

findings.title: S3 bucket has cross-region replication configured to a bucket in a different region
findings.description:
**Amazon S3 buckets** use **cross-Region replication** with `versioning` and an enabled rule that targets a destination bucket in a different AWS Region.

Buckets with same-Region targets, missing destinations, or disabled `versioning` don't meet this replication posture.
findings.recommendation:
Enable **CRR** to a different Region with `versioning` and least-privilege roles.

- Replicate needed prefixes and metadata
- Consider `S3 Replication Time Control` for tighter RPO
- Protect deletes via `delete marker` strategy and Object Lock
- Monitor replication metrics and test DR regularly

Align with **defense in depth** and availability by design.

findings.title: S3 bucket policy does not allow public write access
findings.description: **Amazon S3 bucket policies** are evaluated for **public write permissions** (e.g., `s3:PutObject`, `s3:Delete*`, or `s3:*`). Account or bucket **Public Access Block** that restricts public buckets is considered when determining exposure.
findings.recommendation: Restrict writes to trusted principals using **least privilege**; avoid `Principal: "*"`. Enable **Public Access Block** at account and bucket levels for defense in depth. Prefer IAM roles over broad bucket policies, require private access paths, and enable versioning to recover from unwanted changes.

findings.title: S3 bucket is not publicly accessible to Everyone or Authenticated Users
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **public access** via ACLs and bucket policies. The check identifies account or bucket `PublicAccessBlock` protections (`IgnorePublicAcls`, `RestrictPublicBuckets`) and flags buckets granting group access to `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers`, or with a public bucket policy.
findings.recommendation: Enforce defense in depth: enable **S3 Block Public Access** at org/account and bucket levels (`BlockPublicAcls`, `IgnorePublicAcls`, `BlockPublicPolicy`, `RestrictPublicBuckets`). Apply **least privilege** with explicit principals; avoid ACLs via Object Ownership. Use private access patterns (e.g., CloudFront OAC or presigned URLs) and monitor with analyzers.

findings.title: S3 bucket is not publicly listable by Everyone or any authenticated AWS user
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for **public listing via ACLs**. Grants of `READ`, `READ_ACP`, or `FULL_CONTROL` to the `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers` groups are identified. Effective **Block Public Access** at account or bucket level (notably `IgnorePublicAcls` and `RestrictPublicBuckets`) is considered in the evaluation.
findings.recommendation:
Enable account-level **S3 Block Public Access** (`BlockPublicAcls`, `IgnorePublicAcls`, `BlockPublicPolicy`, `RestrictPublicBuckets`).
- Remove ACL grants to `AllUsers`/`AuthenticatedUsers`; apply **least privilege** with IAM/bucket policies.
- Favor private patterns (VPC endpoints, CloudFront OAC, presigned URLs) and disable ACLs via Object Ownership.

findings.title: S3 bucket ACL does not grant write access to Everyone or any AWS customer
findings.description: **Amazon S3 buckets** are assessed for ACL grants that allow **public write** access to `AllUsers` or `AuthenticatedUsers` via `WRITE`, `WRITE_ACP`, or `FULL_CONTROL`. Effective **Block Public Access** at account or bucket level (`ignore_public_acls`, `restrict_public_buckets`) is considered.
findings.recommendation: Apply **least privilege** to S3 writes. Enable account-level **Block Public Access** and use **Object Ownership** to disable ACLs. Grant write only to fixed principals via bucket policies with tight conditions (e.g., org IDs, VPC endpoints). Add **versioning** and monitoring for defense-in-depth.

findings.title: S3 bucket policy denies requests over insecure transport
findings.description:
**Amazon S3 buckets** are evaluated for a bucket policy that enforces **secure transport** by denying requests when `aws:SecureTransport` is `false`.

Buckets without this explicit denial, or without a policy, are treated as allowing access over insecure transport.
findings.recommendation:
Enforce **HTTPS-only** access with a bucket policy that denies requests when `aws:SecureTransport=false`.

Prefer **private access** (VPC endpoints or CloudFront with TLS), avoid S3 website endpoints, apply **least privilege**, use short-lived HTTPS **pre-signed URLs**, and monitor logs for insecure access attempts.

findings.title: S3 bucket is not a known shadow resource owned by another account
findings.description:
**Amazon S3 buckets** using **predictable service naming** (e.g., `aws-glue-assets-<account>-<region>`, `sagemaker-<region>-<account>`) are identified and their **ownership** checked.

Buckets tied to your account that match these patterns but are owned by another account-across regions-are surfaced as shadow-resource candidates.
findings.recommendation:
Apply **defense in depth**:
- **Preprovision and own** required service buckets in all current and planned regions
- Enforce **least privilege** so services write only to approved bucket names/ARNs
- Use **non-guessable names** where you control naming
- Monitor for look-alike buckets and separate duties for bucket creation vs. use
